title: Inches to Millimeters Converter
shortDescription: Convert inches to millimeters with a fixed 25.4 multiplier and clear decimal results.
metaDescription: Convert inches to millimeters using the fixed 25.4 factor. Get clear decimal results for dimensions, parts, and drawings.
intro: Enter an inch value and get the millimeter equivalent immediately using 1 inch = 25.4 mm.
overview:
This converter turns inch measurements into millimeters using the standard exact factor of 25.4. It is useful when you need a precise metric value from a fractional, decimal, or whole-inch input.

Use it for dimensions from drawings, hardware sizes, material specs, or packaged products that list measurements in inches. The result is shown in millimeters so you can compare values in a metric workflow without doing the multiplication yourself.
howItWorks: Type an inch value into the input field.
The tool multiplies the value by 25.4.
Read the result in millimeters and copy it into your notes, spec sheet, or calculation.

useCases.title: Machine part dimensions
useCases.description: Convert a shaft, bracket, or panel size from inch-based drawings into millimeters for fabrication or inspection.

useCases.title: Product specification sheets
useCases.description: Translate imported item dimensions listed in inches into metric units for catalogs, labels, or purchasing records.

useCases.title: Woodworking and shop setup
useCases.description: Check fence positions, bit sizes, or clearance gaps when a project uses inch measurements but your tools read in millimeters.

useCases.title: Technical drawings and CAD notes
useCases.description: Match inch-based dimensions to a metric workspace when reviewing plans, revisions, or annotation values.

examples.title: Decimal input
examples.description: A decimal inch value converted directly into millimeters.

examples.title: Whole number input
examples.description: Useful for larger measurements from parts, pipes, or panels.

examples.title: Fraction-style value
examples.description: Converts three-quarters of an inch into a metric result.

faq.question: What formula does this converter use?
faq.answer: It uses the exact relation 1 inch = 25.4 millimeters, so the inch value is multiplied by 25.4.

faq.question: Can I enter decimals or fractions?
faq.answer: Decimals are accepted directly. If your source value is written as a fraction, convert it to decimal form first, such as 1/2 = 0.5.

faq.question: Why does the result sometimes show decimals?
faq.answer: Many inch values do not convert to a whole number of millimeters. The decimal part is part of the exact metric equivalent.

faq.question: What is a common mistake when reading the result?
faq.answer: Do not round too early. For parts, drawings, or fit checks, keep the full value until you know how many digits you need.

faq.question: Does this tool change inches into centimeters too?
faq.answer: No. It converts inches to millimeters only. For centimeters, divide the millimeter result by 10.
